There is another group of 120 comets known as Vifaarupa,
born of Agni (Fire). Their forms are filled with rows of flames.
They produce terrible fire-disasters.
[Cf. *\A quoted under verse 11 supra, and instead of the 4th.
line have the following :
Pref ii
From the different numbers of the two sets of comets born of
Agni it is clear that they are not identical. The former set has no
specific name, while the latter is called Visvarupa.]

There are 77 comets, called Aruna, born of the Wind, which
are dark-red in colour, rough, without any siany fcrm, but lecking
like the chowries and possessed of scattered rays. They produce
disastrous consequences.

There are 8 comets known as Ganaka, sons of Prajapati, that
look like a cluster of stars. There are 204 comets named Caturasra
